name: delete_file
content: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/Users/bhupatejassingh/Siri/test.txt' occured durin delting file /Users/bhupatejassingh/Siri/test.txt

I don't see a file named "test.txt" in your working directory. Please confirm if the file exists before attempting to delete it.

If you're sure, you can use the `rm` command to delete the file. Since it doesn't exist, you won't see any output.

```bash
rm /Users/bhupatejassingh/Siri/test.txt
```

However, if you want to delete a non-existent file, you can use the following command with the `-f` (force) and `-i` (interactive) options:

```bash
rm -fi /Users/bhupatejassingh/Siri/test.txt
```

This will prompt you to confirm whether you want to delete the file.

If you want to delete all files in the current directory, you can use the `rm` command with the `*` wildcard:

```bash
rm *
```
